title: College at the University of Hawaii | What it's ACTUALLY like (watch before applying)
body: As someone who attended UH-Manoa nearly 35 years ago, I can confirm there has been no major improvements to the freshman dorms.  Those dorms are HOT as hell!  And if your room is on the west side of a tower, you will bake in the afternoon sun.  On hot and humid nights, those dorm rooms are miserable.
author: @user-sg8kq7ii3y

title: College at the University of Hawaii | What it's ACTUALLY like (watch before applying)
body:
Facilities need major upgrades, I played ball for Hawaii years ago.
I still miss aloha stadium hope the campus gets upgrades and the stadium can be put back into action one day. Simple things like that would attract more students not just student-athletes
author: @8YearHiatus

title: College at the University of Hawaii | What it's ACTUALLY like (watch before applying)
body: I went to UH for 6 years total graduating with 2 degrees. The worse thing about UH is parking. I don’t want to pay for the expensive UH parking so I parked far away sometimes 1 miles away. Mind you It took sometimes 30 minutes to find parking then the walk would be about 1 mile so it would take me at least 15-20 minutes to my class.
author: @apexsw20trd

title: College at the University of Hawaii | What it's ACTUALLY like (watch before applying)
body: Went to UH back in the mid to late 80s. The towers then weren’t coed floors. Not sure if they still have food trucks on campus, but those were awesome. Gateway would have late night snacks/meals and was pretty much always busy. Kanikapila at the amphitheater was always a fun time. One downside was definitely the bomb threats people would call in during finals week. Imagine being woken up at 2am and have to stay outside for at least an hour before going back…worse when it was called in to all 4 towers in one night.  Crazy but fun times.
author: @wendy1823

title: the REALITY of going to school in hawai'i (university of manoa Q&A, what I wish I knew)
body: 14:19 - there are several reasons why there's not a whole lot going on at UH Manoa, especially at night. First and foremost, the vast majority of students, especially local students, must work, at least part-time, and a good number of them full-time, in order to help support themselves, pay tuition, etc. When I attended UH Manoa, I was carrying a full course load, living on my own, and I had to work a lot of hours in order to pay my rent, tuition, books, etc. I find it incredible of how there are college students out there who just go to school, and they got a nice place to live, a car, spending money, etc., and their parents pay for all of it. My grades may have not been the best, but considering I had to work anywhere from 19-30 hrs per week, keep up with my school work, pay for my own tuition, and support myself, I had the best real world education that life could have offered. It was an education in reality of how surviving in the world would be.
author: @user-sg8kq7ii3y

title: What I wish I knew before I committed... | University of Hawaii at Manoa |
body:
protip: university of hawai'i community college campuses give you the same lower div credits for a fraction of the cost as the uni (double check transfer credits tho)
plus it all shows up in your STAR and Laulima stuff
UH Hilo and West O'ahu are great for different types (big island is a different vibe and west has smaller class sizes, more catered to working students + uses the rail) but west has no dorms and is a pretty dead campus
author: @Jolt_of_Justice

title: What I wish I knew before I committed... | University of Hawaii at Manoa |
body: A couple more things about the weather here.  First, the weather on one part of the Island does not indicate what the weather is like in other parts of the Island.  I used to live just outside of Waikiki.  My wife's family lived in Mililani.  We would make plans to go to the beach and they would call to ask if we should do something else because it was raining.  I would look outside and it would be clear and sunny. If you have plans, check the weather where you are going - not where you are.  Second, have you experienced rain while the sun is shining?  I moved here from Pennsylvania just before I started high school (and came back after college in California).  I'll never forget the first time I was out walking, it was bright and sunny, and it started raining.  The rain was just a drizzle but it was so nice and cooling.  At first I thought that someone had their sprinklers on but no, it was rain.  It's a wonderful phenomenon.
author: @fgodek1964

author: @gudetamababygirl

title: What I wish I knew before I committed... | University of Hawaii at Manoa |
body:
It's interesting comparing your experience with mine. I attended UH Manoa from 2011-2014 and stayed in Hale Kuahine. In comparison to the points you brought up:

1.) I had a hard time figuring out how to join student campus activities, but it was always very clear when school started and ended. That was on the academic calendar.

2.) I never really got cold in Hawaii, but I moved there from a colder part of the country.

3.) The bathrooms were co-ed when I was there too, but they were always pristine the entire three years that I lived there.

4.) I really liked the food at the two lower campus cafeterias and ate there everyday until they banned trays. I got really annoyed about not being able to easily carry all the tiny plates they insisted that everyone use instead of trays, so I started buying food off campus instead.

5.) As far as I know, everyone in my building had double rooms with the furniture for two people, but there was only one person assigned to each room, so I never actually had a roommate. That made it really convenient when my friends and parents visited from the mainland since they had a place to stay.

6.) I used the bus to get to and from the airport, but I otherwise found it very inconvenient to use. I walked and biked to get around. The furthest extent was walking to Aina Haina and Bishop Museum, respectively, and biking to Kaneohe and Kailua. I also hiked over the mountains from Manoa Falls to Judd Trail and then took the roads back.

7.) I agree on the storage. I accumulated lots of books while I was there since I didn't sell my textbooks between semesters and I also like to read. An inconvenience was needing to pack all my stuff into storage closets on other floors between semesters whenever I left to visit my parents and then packing it back into my room when I came back. My dorm building didn't have elevators, so it was difficult moving all my books up and down the stairs at the end and start of each semester.

8.) Socially, I found it difficult to meet people on campus, but there was a board game group that would meet sometimes in that little building near Hale Manoa (I forget its name) and there are hiking trails that lead from Dole Street up to Waʻahila Ridge, which I learned about from people in my dorm. It was fun to go hiking up there, but don't do it after it rains because the red mud will really stick to your feet and make your feet heavier with each step!
author: @ARKdeEREH

title: COLLEGE MOVE IN DAY VLOG 2023 @ University of Hawaii !! | freshman year ep. 01
body: When I first came to Hawaii. I did not live in a dorm because I was in 3rd grade in Elementary school at the time of the move to Hawaii for me and my parents.  But I did live in two houses on base in Hawaii on Hickam.  One was on Kiele Place which was a ranch house. The one nice thing  was  I had a private alleyway to my own swing and I was able to walk and from my school Nimitz Elementary since this house was so close to my school in Hawaii but my parents had to deal with roaches in this house. Then later I moved to Gemini Avenue with my parents in the summer of 2006 just before I went into 4th grade that year.

title: University of Hawai'i at Mānoa Campus Highlights
body:
That’s great news—and it’s encouraging to see arts and humanities recognized alongside the sciences and law. Rankings matter, but what really sustains excellence is how knowledge and skill are formed in people, not just measured.

That belief is what led me to write The Rhythm of Three Strings: An Island Blues Cigar Box Guitar Odyssey. It’s a coming-of-age story set on a sunlit island where a boy grows through mentorship, discipline, and craftsmanship—learning life the old way, by building a simple cigar box guitar with two Navy veterans and carrying forward what was passed down to him. Different scale than a university, same foundation: learning rooted in tradition, effort, and human guidance.

Congrats to UH Mānoa on the recognition.
author: @AHerosJourneyAdventureSeries

title: University of Hawai'i at Mānoa Campus Highlights
body: I attended UH-Manoa for 1 year back in the late 1970s....worked at SSRI....and met people from all over the Pacific....a life changer for me.....had experiences on the mainland with civil rights/riots.....but a different experience at UH.....working with lepers who were publishing their life experiences thru SSRI was a unique experience....bless them all.....more mainland people need to attend UH ...even for only a semester or two....to broaden their view of the world...thank you UH-Manoa for making me a more aware person.....
author: @anniechaloupka8052

With my GPA of 3.59, and the skeleton support to stand up today that I couldn't do before, I am not shy anymore, but it's yet to be tested. I'm ready.

title: University of Hawai'i at Mānoa Campus Highlights
body:
December 6, 2025

Irene Imanil here to inform you that you may be seeing me in the campus, walking to the cafeteria or to Hamilton Library trying to brush up on Physics. I wonder who are the engineers and physicists I can talk to about the deeper general properties of light, that is to locate electron's position at a given time and then blow it up. Is this possible? I wonder how many electrons are clumped up in the electron cloud--the greatest space to find? What if we attract all these electrons and blow them all up? Is this feasible?

I was a product of UH Manoa, but no cash on my ebt. I wonder if UH at Manoa can by pass the requirement of Cyrca, soon to be launched as anti-psychotic prescription medications much like Seroquel.

Finally, let's come up with the sequel of A beautiful mind beauty with my appearance at UH at Manoa at my advancing age. I'm sure many of you have watched the movie of A beautiful mind.

I ask and I want you to rally on my behalf for a support to release my US Passport to me as an ID so the registrar's office will release the paid transcript. Don't do the theft that other women did so I won't be block. I am a target of heist. Help me and my siblings, nieces and nephews (loved ones) escape death.

Irene.

title: UH Manoa (College in Hawaii) Campus Tour
body: Graduated in 1988, lived in Gateway, the round dorms and East West Center Hale Manoa, best times of my life, it was a blast.    I worked the front desk at Lincoln Hall, Hale Kuahine and the main Easr West Center dorm.  I loved when we had tropical storms and from my dorm window I could hear the stream rushing with water coming from Manoa Valley. I used to walk to that Longs Drugs in Manoa Valley too for snacks.😊
author: @ikaikamaleko8370
